title: Neural correlates of metacognitive ability and of feeling confident: a large-scale fMRI study.

assets.abstract: One important aspect of metacognition is the ability to accurately evaluate one's performance. People vary widely in their metacognitive ability and in general are too confident when evaluating their performance. This often leads to poor decision making with potentially disastrous consequences. To further our understanding of the neural underpinnings of these processes, this fMRI study investigated inter-individual differences in metacognitive ability and effects of trial-by-trial variation in s

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
